1. Main heading: What is WDR5?
2. Paragraph: A clear starting point for families, clinicians and any?
fields.value: A clear starting point for families, clinicians and anyone learning about WDR5-related neurodevelopmental conditions.

fields.label: 3. Section heading: About the WDR5 gene
4. Paragraph: WDR5 is a gene that provides instructions for a protein?
fields.value: WDR5 is a gene that provides instructions for a protein involved in regulating how other genes are switched on and off. Changes affecting this gene can disrupt typical development and may be associated with a range of neurodevelopmental features.

fields.label: 5. Paragraph: Because WDR5-related conditions are rare, knowledge is ?
fields.value: Because WDR5-related conditions are rare, knowledge is still developing. Every person is different, and the combination and intensity of features can vary considerably.

fields.label: 6. Section heading: Features families may encounter
7. List item: Developmental delay or intellectual disability
8. List item: Differences in speech, language and communication
fields.value: Differences in speech, language and communication

fields.label: 9. List item: Motor, coordination or muscle-tone differences
10. List item: Feeding, sleep, sensory or behavioral challenges
fields.value: Feeding, sleep, sensory or behavioral challenges

fields.label: 11. List item: Medical features that require individual specialist rev?
fields.value: Medical features that require individual specialist review

fields.label: 12. Paragraph: This information is educational and should not replace ?
fields.value: This information is educational and should not replace advice from a qualified healthcare professional who knows the individual.

fields.label: 13. Section heading: Diagnosis and next steps
14. Paragraph: A diagnosis is usually made through genetic testing, of?
fields.value: A diagnosis is usually made through genetic testing, often after an assessment by a clinical genetics or neurodevelopmental team. After diagnosis, families may be offered further medical checks, therapy referrals and ongoing developmental support based on individual needs.

fields.label: 15. Card/subheading: You do not have to navigate this alone.
fields.value: You do not have to navigate this alone.

fields.label: 16. Paragraph: Join the family directory, meet others with shared expe?
fields.value: Join the family directory, meet others with shared experience and receive carefully selected information as knowledge grows.

18. Card/subheading: Share knowledge
19. Paragraph: We welcome contact from clinicians and researchers inte?
fields.value: We welcome contact from clinicians and researchers interested in collaboration, natural history and family-informed research.

fields.value: Three helpful first steps for families beginning their WDR5 journey.

fields.label: 23. Card/subheading: Build your care team
24. Paragraph: Work with genetics, developmental, therapy and medical ?
fields.value: Work with genetics, developmental, therapy and medical specialists to create a plan around the individual.

fields.label: 25. Card/subheading: Record what matters
26. Paragraph: Keep reports, test results and questions together so ap?
fields.value: Keep reports, test results and questions together so appointments and referrals are easier to coordinate.

fields.label: 27. Card/subheading: Connect with others
28. Paragraph: Shared experience can offer practical ideas, reassuranc?
fields.value: Shared experience can offer practical ideas, reassurance and a sense of belonging.

fields.label: 29. Section heading: WDR5 FAQ
30. FAQ question: How rare are WDR5-related conditions?
31. FAQ answer: They are currently considered very rare, and the number?
fields.value: They are currently considered very rare, and the number of diagnosed individuals is likely to grow as genetic testing becomes more widely available.

fields.label: 32. FAQ question: Will every person have the same features?
fields.value: Will every person have the same features?

fields.label: 33. FAQ answer: No. Genetic conditions can affect people differently. C?
fields.value: No. Genetic conditions can affect people differently. Care and support should always be based on the individual's strengths, needs and clinical findings.

fields.label: 34. FAQ question: Is research underway?
35. FAQ answer: Yes. Researchers are continuing to study WDR5 biology, ?
fields.value: Yes. Researchers are continuing to study WDR5 biology, collect clinical information and explore how better knowledge may support future care and treatment strategies.

fields.label: 36. FAQ question: How can our family help research?
fields.value: How can our family help research?

fields.label: 37. FAQ answer: Register with the foundation and choose to receive rese?
fields.value: Register with the foundation and choose to receive research opportunities. Participation should always be voluntary, informed and reviewed through appropriate ethics processes.

fields.label: 1. Main heading: Together, possibility grows.
2. Paragraph: We unite families, clinicians, researchers and advocate?
fields.value: We unite families, clinicians, researchers and advocates around a shared commitment to knowledge, support and hope.

fields.label: 3. Section heading: Our mission
4. Paragraph: The WDR5 Foundation exists to improve life for people a?
fields.value: The WDR5 Foundation exists to improve life for people affected by WDR5-related conditions. We build trusted connections, make knowledge easier to access, elevate family experience and accelerate responsible research.

fields.label: 5. Section heading: Our vision
6. Paragraph: A world where every person with a WDR5-related conditio?
fields.value: A world where every person with a WDR5-related condition is understood, supported and able to benefit from the best possible care and scientific progress.

fields.label: 7. Section heading: How we work
8. Paragraph: Rare-disease progress is strongest when families and pr?
fields.value: Rare-disease progress is strongest when families and professionals work side by side. We listen first, communicate clearly, protect privacy and invest energy where it can create durable benefit for the whole community.

fields.label: 9. Quote: “No family should have to build the map alone.”
fields.value: “No family should have to build the map alone.”

fields.label: 10. Section heading: Our values
11. Card/subheading: Connection
12. Paragraph: We create spaces where people feel seen, heard and less?
fields.value: We create spaces where people feel seen, heard and less alone.

fields.label: 13. Card/subheading: Trust
14. Paragraph: We communicate honestly and protect the confidence plac?
fields.value: We communicate honestly and protect the confidence placed in us.

fields.label: 15. Card/subheading: Curiosity
16. Paragraph: We keep asking better questions and make room for new e?
fields.value: We keep asking better questions and make room for new evidence.

fields.label: 17. Card/subheading: Hope
18. Paragraph: We pair optimism with practical action and long-term co?
fields.value: We pair optimism with practical action and long-term commitment.

fields.value: Early connections revealed a shared need for reliable information, belonging and a coordinated voice.

fields.value: Community priorities became a practical mission spanning support, education, advocacy and research.

25. Paragraph: Families and professionals across countries are contrib?
fields.value: Families and professionals across countries are contributing knowledge and widening possibilities for the future.

23. Section heading: Choose sources carefully
24. Paragraph: Rare-condition information can change quickly and quali?
fields.value: Rare-condition information can change quickly and quality varies. Prefer resources that identify their authors, cite evidence, show a review date and separate established knowledge from emerging ideas.

fields.value: Check when the resource was last reviewed.
